summary: Iteration 12 testing completed successfully. All 32 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend verification confirmed all features working: PvP Strategy Battle, Social/Copy Trading, Strategy Optimizer, Mobile PWA responsiveness. The new PvP Battle feature correctly runs strategy comparisons across historical events and displays winner with detailed breakdown.
passed_tests: System Status: GET /api/system/status returns running status
Auth Login: POST /api/auth/login with admin credentials works
Auth Login: POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials returns 401
Auth Register: POST /api/auth/register creates new user
PvP Battle: POST /api/social/pvp with two configs returns battle results with per-event breakdown
PvP Battle: POST /api/social/pvp with specific event_ids works
PvP Battle: POST /api/social/pvp without configs returns 400
PvP History: GET /api/social/pvp/history returns list of past battles
Social Leaderboard: GET /api/social/leaderboard returns ranked strategies
Social Share: POST /api/social/share creates and returns shared strategy
Social Follow: POST /api/social/follow/{id} follows strategy and returns config
Social Follow: POST /api/social/follow/{id} toggles to unfollow on second call
Social Follow: POST /api/social/follow/nonexistent returns 404
Social My Strategies: GET /api/social/my-strategies returns user's strategies
Social Following: GET /api/social/following returns followed strategies
Strategy Optimizer: POST /api/replay/optimize returns best config, top_10, total_combinations
Replay Events: GET /api/replay/events returns 8 historical events
Replay Simulate: POST /api/replay/simulate runs strategy simulation
Replay Compare: POST /api/replay/compare runs multi-event comparison
Options Chain: GET /api/options/chain/CL returns options data
Options Straddle: POST /api/options/strategy/straddle creates straddle
Options Strangle: POST /api/options/strategy/strangle creates strangle
Options Iron Condor: POST /api/options/strategy/iron-condor creates iron condor
Options Butterfly: POST /api/options/strategy/butterfly creates butterfly
Options Backtest: POST /api/options/backtest runs options backtest
Bot Status: GET /api/bot/status returns enabled, min_confidence
Bot Opportunities: GET /api/bot/opportunities returns pending opportunities
CSV Export: GET /api/trades/export/csv returns CSV data
Alerts: GET /api/alerts returns alerts list
Alerts: POST /api/alerts creates price alert
Fragility: GET /api/fragility returns score, level
Risk Control: GET /api/risk-control/status returns can_trade status
PWA Manifest: GET /manifest.json is accessible
Frontend: Dashboard tab loads with real-time chart and market data
Frontend: Options tab shows strategy builder with 6 strategy types
Frontend: Replay tab shows Multi-Event Comparison, Strategy Optimizer, Strategy Replay
Frontend: Social tab shows Strategy Leaderboard with Follow buttons
Frontend: Social tab shows PvP Strategy Battle with config inputs and results
Frontend: Settings tab shows Price Alerts and Trade Export
Frontend: Mobile responsive - tabs are scrollable, layout adapts to 390x844 viewport
Frontend: WebSocket connection established (LIVE status)
seed_data_creation: Test strategies created during testing (TEST_Strategy_*, TEST_Optimized_*, TEST_FollowMe_*)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 12 features fully functional. New features verified: 1) PvP Strategy Battle (POST /api/social/pvp) - pits two strategy configs against each other across all historical events, returns per-event breakdown with winner, summary for each strategy, and overall winner. Frontend shows config inputs for Strategy A and B, Start Battle button, and results with VS display. 2) Mobile PWA enhancements - tabs are scrollable on mobile, safe-area-bottom class applied, manifest.json accessible. All features from iterations 1-11 continue to work correctly. The codebase uses modular router pattern with /routers/ directory. All API routes prefixed with /api via api_router in server.py.

mocked_apis.note: All trading is simulated via PaperBroker. Market data is simulated. Replay events use pre-defined trajectories. ML regime detection uses Emergent LLM Key.
